Earlier, people believed that: “Seeing is believing.”But in today’s digital world, this is not always true. With advanced technology, videos and images can be edited so realistically that they look completely real—even when they are not. This is where the idea of deepfakes and fake content comes in.What is Fake Content?Fake content is any:Image • Video • Audio • Message that is created or changed to mislead people.Examples: 	• 	A photo edited to show something that never happenedA message with false informationWhat are Deepfakes?Deepfakes are a special type of fake content created using Artificial Intelligence (AI).AI can copy:A person’s face 	•  Their voice 	• Their expressionsand place them into a video where they appear to say or do things they never actually did.Why Are Deepfakes and Fake Content Dangerous?They Can Spread False Information- Fake content can make people believe something that never happened.They Can Harm Someone’s Reputation- A deepfake can make a person appear to do something wrong or embarrassing. This can hurt the person’s dignity, friendships, or public image.They Can Create Confusion- When fake content becomes common, people may begin to doubt even real videos and photos. This creates confusion.They Can Influence People’s Opinions- Fake videos or images can be used to change how people think about a person, event or issue. During important situations, such as elections, public events or emergencies, misleading content can create fear, anger, or wrong decisions.How to Identify Suspicious ContentYou don’t need to be an expert—just observe carefully:Look for: 	• Unnatural facial movements  	 	•  Voice not matching lip movement 	  	• Blurry or distorted areas in videos 	 	•  Strange lighting or shadows
